title: LINE AI Dev
description: Building real-time AI chat UI with streaming responses and robust networking
responsibilities: Architected iOS client module handling response streaming, conversation state, and network/cache optimization for hundreds of millions of DAUs
Implemented URLSession-based Server-Sent Events (SSE) streaming for real-time AI responses
Developed Markdown Parser and NSAttributedString-based Renderer for rich text display
Defined robust error, retry, and cancellation policies for unreliable network conditions
Coordinated API contract alignment with backend engineers and product managers
detailedDescription: Leading iOS development for LINE's in-app AI assistant. Deep expertise in networking protocols (URLSession, SSE), asynchronous programming (Combine, async/await), and building responsive UIs that handle streaming data at scale.

title: Immersive Memory — Vision Pro (LY HACK DAY Award)
description: Award-winning visionOS experience with SwiftUI and RealityKit
responsibilities: Built visionOS experience for reliving shared memories in a 3D gallery using SwiftUI
Placed photos in RealityKit 3D scene with gaze- and gesture-driven interaction
Integrated PhotoKit and AVFoundation for media asset handling
Won Communication Award at LY Corporation's internal Hack Day in Japan
detailedDescription: Designed and built a visionOS experience that lets LINE users relive shared memories in a 3D gallery using Apple Vision Pro. Demonstrated expertise in SwiftUI, RealityKit, and Apple's latest spatial UI frameworks. Recognized for immediate integration viability into LINE service.

title: LINE Voom Camera (iOS)
description: Led AVFoundation-based camera with real-time effects and multi-track video composition
responsibilities: Owned VoomCameraViewController — AVFoundation capture session, live preview rendering, and device-specific format selection
Designed real-time preview effects pipeline using Metal and Core Image
Architected Storyboard data model with NSCoding and schema versioning for full editing state
Built VOOMVideoComposer for multi-track video/audio composition using AVMutableComposition
Ensured thread safety using GCD and OperationQueue; optimized memory for large multi-track edits
detailedDescription: Led the iOS team for LINE's short-form video product shipped to global users (JP/TW/TH). Deep expertise in UIKit, AVFoundation, Metal, and asynchronous programming with GCD/OperationQueue. Delivered polished camera UI with real-time effects and robust video editing capabilities.

title: LINE Avatar (iOS)
description: Face-driven avatar creation with camera capture and Vision framework
responsibilities: Launched LINE Avatar (iOS) in 2019 — face recognition SDK integration and camera capture/processing
Implemented avatar customization, catalog, and preview UI
Integrated with messenger chat and profile interfaces
Introduced MVVM architecture pattern (2020-2022) improving testability and reducing UI coupling
detailedDescription: Developed LINE Avatar feature for iOS — face recognition-based character creation, customization, and sharing. Built camera capture screens with Vision/CoreImage-based face detection. First production project at LINE Plus, demonstrating strong OOP and architecture design skills.

title: RGB-D Hand Gesture Recognition (C++)
description: Real-time 21-joint hand skeleton extraction from RGB-D camera at 30fps
responsibilities: Implemented OpenNI depth stream initialization and resolution/FPS configuration
Developed depth-first hand segmentation with threshold, noise removal, and hole filling
Built RGB-Depth alpha blending pipeline for frame composition
Extracted 21-joint skeleton using Convolutional Pose Machines
Achieved 91% accuracy with 3x performance improvement (6fps → 18fps)
detailedDescription: Graduation thesis project using Intel RealSense SR300 RGB-D camera. Built real-time image processing pipeline targeting 30fps — depth-first segmentation, RGB-Depth alpha blending, and 21-joint skeleton extraction. Normalized joint distance data to handle varying camera distances, improving accuracy to 91%.
publication: Inha University Computer Science Graduation Thesis

title: Unity 2.5D Adventure Game
description: 2.5D adventure with scene transitions, inventory, and puzzles
responsibilities: Designed game system and implemented core scripts
Set up character, camera, and lighting
Implemented 2.5D camera rotation and water shader effects
detailedDescription: 2D camera with 3D character placement — a 2.5D adventure game built in Unity. Includes scene transitions, inventory system, and simple puzzles. Capstone project for game programming coursework.

title: Roller Coaster Graphics (OpenGL · C++)
description: 3D roller coaster with Catmull-Rom spline track and real-time camera
responsibilities: Implemented Catmull-Rom spline-based track curve generation
Built 1st/3rd person camera switching system
Applied texture mapping and lighting effects
detailedDescription: OpenGL-based 3D roller coaster track and camera system. Implemented curve interpolation, texture mapping, and lighting. C++ real-time rendering experience from computer graphics coursework.

title: EDEN — IoT Theme Park Management
description: IoT-based theme park management with LPWA communication
responsibilities: Wrote sensor data collection firmware and communication protocol
Organized mobile/server integration interfaces
Implemented NFC payment, queue management, and lost child alerts
detailedDescription: Designed IoT solution to solve theme park problems — payment alerts, inefficient wait times, lost children. Used Low Power Wide Area (LPWA/LoRa) communication to connect small devices across large areas.

title: Cats in your Street
description: Web service for sharing street cats in your neighborhood
responsibilities: Developed web service for cat community
Implemented location-based features
detailedDescription: Developed 'Cats in your Street', a web service for street cat community.

title: LINE Yuki Camera/Graphics Framework
description: Internal camera/graphics framework powering LINE's media features
responsibilities: Joined Yuki camera/graphics framework team supporting LINE's media products
Owned CI/CD pipeline and Jenkins-based framework deployment to internal CocoaPods
Debugged complex Objective-C runtime issues across framework consumers
Maintained backward compatibility for Swift and Objective-C interoperability
Collaborated with multiple product teams (Avatar, Voom, Messenger) on framework integration
detailedDescription: Yuki is LINE's internal camera and graphics framework used across multiple products. Deep experience with framework-level development, Objective-C runtime debugging, and CI/CD automation. This role built strong foundations for later leading product teams that consumed the framework.

responsibilities: 역할/성과 1
역할/성과 2
역할/성과 3
detailedDescription: 프로젝트에 대한 상세 설명을 작성합니다. 여러 줄로 작성해도 됩니다. ``` ### 필수 필드 | 필드 | 설명 | |------|------| | `id` | 고유 ID (영문, 하이픈 사용) | | `title` | 프로젝트 제목 | | `category` | 카테고리 (iOS Development, AR/VR, Research 등) | | `thumbnail` | 썸네일 이미지 URL | | `tags` | 기술 태그 목록 | | `description` | 짧은 설명 | | `role` | 역할 | | `period` | 기간 | ### 선택 필드 | 필드 | 설명 | |------|------| | `contribution` | 기여도 (0-100) | | `publication` | 논문/발표 제목 | | `publicationUrl` | 논문/발표 링크 | | `demoVideo` | 데모 영상 URL | | `event` | 관련 행사명 |
